name: Carrot Cake with Lemon Frosting
recipeIngredient: 1 pound carrots, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1 pound cream cheese, at room temperature
1 1/4 cups unsalted butter, at room temperature
fresh edible flowers (such as pink roses; optional)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Butter 3 (9-by-11/2-inch) round cake pans. Line bottoms with wax paper. Butter the paper.
Cook carrots in a large pot of boiling salted water until tender, about 12 minutes. Drain. Puree in a food processor, stopping occasionally to scrape down sides of bowl. Transfer to a large bowl; cool.
Whisk oil, pineapple, eggs, and vanilla into carrot puree. Mix flour, sugar, cinnamon, soda, and salt in a medium bowl. Stir into puree. Mix in nuts.
Divide batter among prepared pans. Bake until a tester inserted into centers comes out clean, about 35 minutes. Cool cakes in pans on racks. Turn cakes out of pans; peel off wax paper. Place one layer on a platter. Spread 3/4 cup frosting over it. Top with second cake; spread 3/4 cup frosting over it. Top with third cake. Spread remaining frosting over top and sides. May be prepared up to 1 day ahead. Cover with cake dome and refrigerate. Let stand 3 hours at room temperature before serving.
Frosting:
With an electric mixer, beat cream cheese, butter, lemon juice, and vanilla until light and fluffy. Gradually beat in sugar. Chill until firm but spreadable, about 1 hour.
